title: Use XML Tags for Structure
description: Claude parses XML natively — prefer XML tags over markdown headers
rule: Wrap prompt sections in XML tags for Claude: use <context> for background, <instructions> for the task, <example> for demonstrations, and <constraints> for limits. Claude parses XML natively and performs better with tagged structure than with markdown headers or triple-quoted blocks.

title: Use Markdown Headers and Positive Framing
description: ChatGPT responds well to markdown structure and literal positive instructions
rule: Use markdown headers (##) or triple-quoted blocks ("""...""") to delimit sections for ChatGPT. GPT-4+ models follow literal instructions closely — be precise. Frame all instructions positively ('be concise', 'only use provided context') rather than negatively ('don't be verbose', 'don't make things up').

title: Use Markdown and System Instructions
description: Gemini responds well to markdown formatting and front-loaded system instructions
rule: Use markdown formatting for Gemini. Place background context and system-level instructions at the very beginning of the prompt — Gemini attends most strongly to content at the start. For multi-turn or long tasks, provide a completion prefix (a partial response) to guide the model's output format and tone.
